| | Peter,

Last night I took an Uber ride with Laura, a teacher who drives after work on some weeknights and also on weekends to help make ends meet. During the day, she teaches elementary students at risk of falling behind.

She's also featured in our latest TV ad. subject: Read the White House’s official response to Speaker Pelosi and Democrat leaders
body:
This evening, the White House sent an eight-page letter to Speaker Nancy Pelosi and other House Democrat leaders... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=02710a544c&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • October 8, 2019
---
| | |
# **Read the White House’s official response to Speaker Pelosi and Democrat leaders**

**This evening,** the White House sent an eight-page letter to Speaker Nancy Pelosi and other House Democrat leaders, responding to the unprecedented, unconstitutional “impeachment inquiry” launched against President Donald J. Trump.

**The letter** , written by White House Counsel Pat Cipollone, lays out how Democrat leaders designed a politically motivated inquiry that violates the constitutionally mandated due process protections:
| | _You have denied the President the right to cross-examine witnesses, to call witnesses, to receive transcripts of testimony, to have access to evidence, to have counsel present, and many other basic rights guaranteed to all Americans. You have conducted your proceedings in secret. You have violated civil liberties and the separation of powers by threatening Executive Branch officials, claiming that you will seek to punish those who exercise fundamental constitutional rights and prerogatives. All of this violates the Constitution, the rule of law, and****_ _every past precedent._ _Never before in our history has the House of Representatives—under the control of either political party—taken the American people down the dangerous path you seem determined to pursue._ |
---|---|---

**The facts are clear.** The House Democrats’ “impeachment inquiry,” undertaken without a single vote of authorization by the full House—an astonishing breach of precedent—is constitutionally invalid and violates both basic due process rights and the separation of powers. By the admission of its own advocates in Congress, it plainly seeks to reverse the election of 2016 and to influence the election of 2020. Not only is there no legitimate basis for the inquiry, but the committees’ actions in pursuing it themselves raise serious questions that demand full transparency with the American people.

[Read the White House’s full response to Speaker Pelosi and Democrat leaders.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e4c15e5bd7&e=3ee4985eea)
|
---
|
# **How President Trump is securing our border—without Congress’ help**

**At a White House briefing today,** Acting Commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection Mark Morgan announced the fourth straight month of a sharp decline in illegal immigrant apprehensions along America’s southwest border.

**In September** , just over 52,000 apprehensions occurred—a stunning improvement from the 144,000 in May.

** ****Acting Commissioner Morgan:** [The President’s actions are working.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0a1f6baaef&e=3ee4985eea)

**How did this happen?** It’s not because Congress finally stepped up after decades of broken promises and inaction. Instead, President Trump combined stricter enforcement of long-neglected laws with tough negotiations between the United States and its neighbors to the south. Today, for example, Mexico is helping to address the flow of illegal caravans that had been marching unimpeded through Mexico to the U.S. border.

**Part of this renewed enforcement,** of course, includes building the wall.

**The work is far from finished,** however. The underlying cause of our country’s immigration crisis—incentives in our laws to circumvent America’s safe, legal, and fair immigration system—are still there. If Congressional Democrats continue to insist on outsourcing control of our borders to criminal smugglers and cartels, both American citizens and migrant families will suffer.

**“The bottom line:** We still need Congress to pass meaningful legislation to address our broken legal framework when it comes to immigration,” Acting Commissioner Morgan said. “And while Congress has failed to bring a single piece of meaningful legislation to the floor, this President and this Administration is doing exactly what he promised to the American people.”

[Watch: The media won’t report it, but our Border Patrol officers are saving lives!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=096f11a8ba&e=3ee4985eea)

** ****Mile by mile:** [We are building the border wall.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3b04f560c8&e=3ee4985eea)
---

name: Team Chabot
subject: Rep. Jim Jordan Endorses Rep. Steve Chabot for Reelection
body:
Today's Democrats have gone to a whole new extreme. Just when we thought they couldn't get further to the left, today's Democrats are now calling for widespread socialism, universal healthcare, the Green New Deal, slavery reparations, open borders, and even infanticide. No Images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-jdtiydtd-kujkulhluu-t/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jdtiydtd-kujkulhluu-r/) **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE**Tuesday October 8, 2019CONTACT: Jon ConradiPHONE: ‪(513) 202-4023** _Rep. Jim Jordan Endorses Rep. Steve Chabot for Reelection_** _Leading Conservative House Republican Backs Ohio Colleague in 2020_ CINCINNATI - Rep. Jim Jordan (R-OH-04), a leading conservative House Republican, endorsed Rep. Steve Chabot (R-OH-01) for reelection during a campaign event at the Great Wolf Lodge in Mason, Ohio Monday evening."Thank you for sending Steve Chabot to Congress," Jordan told a crowd of more than five dozen Chabot supporters in Warren County on Monday. "He's got the right values, the right principles, the right tenacity. I mean, he's the kind of guy you want going to fight for you and your family and your business, your principles in the United States Congress."Chabot and Jordan serve separately as the Republican leaders of two committees and serve together on the House Committee on the Judiciary. Chabot is the Ranking Member of the House Committee on Small Business. Jordan is the Ranking Member of the House Committee on Oversight and Reform."I'm proud to have the support of my friend and colleague Congressman Jordan," Chabot said. "Jordan is a fierce advocate for conservative solutions and invaluable fighter against the left's impeachment hysteria in Congress." | [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jdtiydtd-kujkulhluu-y/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jdtiydtd-kujkulhluu-j/)

name: Jill Vogel
subject: You Are Invited To The Atoka Country Supper!
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Senate 45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100 Warrenton, VA 20186
body:
Please join Alex & I as we bring back this Historic event!
---
| | | | | |
---
| | |
---
| Friends,
I am delighted to announce that Senator John Warner joins me in the revival of his legendary Akota Country Supper. His annual event promoted civility, fellowship and a strong sense of community among neighbors and friends for a generation. It was made famous by his guests President Ronald and Nancy Reagan, President George H.W. Bush, and Warner's wife actress Elizabeth Taylor. He has been eager to have us bring back that fun event and has given me wonderful articles, photos and memorabilia to share. Please join us to be with friends, hear from Senator Warner, and enjoy music and Shaffer's BBQ - Sunday, October 27th at our home, 3:30 pm to 6:00 pm!

Friends-- We are just weeks away from November's election that will determine control of the Virginia General Assembly, and there is big news out this week. Both Christopher Newport University and the _Washington Post _have polls out that show Virginia is ready for a Democratic majority and that we have the lead! These polls also show that Governor Northam is in a strong position to lead the Commonwealth forward over the next two years. [**But, we need your help to finish the job. Every dollar you contribute today will help candidates with late television buys and our effort to turn out the vote in November.**](https://act.ralphnortham.com/page/m/296f21df/39260142/42782f03/6263955e/3907355260/VEsE/?g=0cliBlH8x8VbPwb7zJ42YiA "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/vamajority2019") Under Governor Northam's leadership, Virginia has accomplished so much including expanding Medicaid for approximately 400,000 Virginians. And, there is so much more we can do in the next two years with a Democratic majority. Over 1,000 Virginians lost their lives to gun violence last year. It is long past time to take action to put an end to gun violence. [**The polls have us ahead but we can 't take anything for granted. Stand with us today by making a contribution of $5, $10, $25 or $50 toward our effort.**](https://act.ralphnortham.com/page/m/296f21df/39260142/42782f03/6263955e/3907355260/VEsF/?g=0cliBlH8x8VbPwb7zJ42YiA "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/vamajority2019") Thank you for everything you have done to help us pave the way ahead for Virginia. subject: Why Can’t Dems Treat Trump as Fairly as Republicans Did Bill Clinton?
body:
The White House informed House Speaker Nancy Pelosi yesterday that "the Executive Branch won’t play along with the lawless ‘inquiry’ that..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b48ea9e681&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Why Can’t Dems Treat Trump as Fairly as Republicans Did Bill Clinton?**

The White House informed House Speaker Nancy Pelosi yesterday that “the Executive Branch won’t play along with the lawless ‘inquiry’ that House Democrats have been engaged in,” the _New York Post_ editorial board writes.

“White House counsel Pat Cipollone’s letter to Pelosi spells out the problems. While the Constitution clearly gives the House the power to begin impeachment proceedings, it does not give the speaker the privilege of declaring them all by herself.”

Precedent is on President Trump’s side here, the _Post_ writes. Unlike impeachment inquiries against Presidents Bill Clinton and Richard Nixon, Speaker Pelosi never called a floor vote to open proceedings. Instead, House Democrats are deposing witnesses behind closed doors, denying Republican colleagues fair time to ask questions, and “leaking negative info and withholding favorable facts — feeding fanatically anti-Trump media to repeat slanted interpretations as fact.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d07fb76f53&e=3ee4985eea)

**The letter:**[Read the White House’s full response to Democrat leaders.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3b84579c9c&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| Once again, the media got a key detail wrong about the “whistleblower” attack against President Trump. “The whistleblower's possible bias was not that he was simply a registered Democrat. It was that he had a significant tie to one of the Democratic presidential candidates currently vying to challenge President Trump in next year's election,” Byron York writes in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5a3792befc&e=3ee4985eea) _._
---
|
---
| “First lady Melania Trump announced on Tuesday afternoon that she had—ceremonial shovel in hand—marked the start of construction for a new White House tennis pavilion on the South Lawn,” Kate Bennett reports for [**CNN**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9a50ba22b9&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “President Trump awarded the Medal of Freedom Tuesday to Edwin Meese III, calling the former attorney general ‘a titan’ and ‘a star,’” Marisa Schultz reports for the [_New York Post_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b62a65460f&e=3ee4985eea). Meese served as U.S. Attorney General under President Ronald Reagan.
---
|

When we show that 2 million small-dollar contributions can compete -- and win -- we'll send a message to wealthy donors that they no longer have a stranglehold on our democracy.

And when we hit 2 million, we will know how we got there. With zero contributions from closed-door fundraisers, zero from fossil fuel lobbyists, and zero from billionaires buying favors.

[1]Beto for America

This week marks a pivotal moment in the fight for LGBTQ equality.

Yesterday, the Supreme Court began hearing arguments from three cases to
determine whether it’s legal to fire workers for being LGBTQ.

During the Obama administration, the Equal Employment Opportunity
Commission took the stance that federal law protects LGBTQ Americans from
workplace discrimination. And now, the Trump administration is arguing to
reverse this position.

Let’s make this abundantly clear: No person should be fired for who they
are or who they love. Beto is proud to be fighting for LGBTQ equality and
has promised to make this fight a priority in our campaign.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/37790?refcode=ema20191009-60278&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1019&akid=60278%2E444211%2E_OirJK ]Will you chip in $3 or more to help elect a leader that
is committed to protecting the full rights of every single one of our
fellow Americans?

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/37790?refcode=ema20191009-60278&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=60278%2E444211%2E_OirJK ]Contribute $3

What makes this even worse is that this is only one example of the string
of attacks this bigoted administration has launched on the LGBTQ
community.

* After 1 month in office, Trump withdrew federal guidance that protects
the rights of transgender students to access bathrooms and locker
rooms that match their gender identity.
* In April, Trump’s transgender military ban officially went into
effect.
* He pushed an agenda to allow businesses to turn away LGBTQ customers
under a religious exemption.
* He sought to eliminate LGBTQ healthcare discrimination protections.

And this is just the beginning.

The bigotry and hatred we’re seeing from this administration simply
doesn’t represent us. That’s why Beto released a comprehensive plan in
June that maps out a plan to achieve equality for all Americans and places
it as a top priority.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/37790?refcode=ema20191009-60278&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=60278%2E444211%2E_OirJK ]Will you chip in $3 to bring Beto’s plan to life and

name: J.D. Scholten
subject: What Meals on Wheels means to me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jdbooker [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jdbooker]
Peter,
Meals on Wheels holds a special place in my heart. My great-grandma was a recipient and relied on their hot, nutritious meals just like many other elderly folks across the 4th district and around the country. But they don't only provide meals, they're also critical emotional and social support, especially in our rural communities, where folks may be far from family or a hospital or grocery store.
That's why I was so honored to join Angela Kofoot, who leads the Meals on Wheels program in Spirit Lake, and Cory Booker to discuss these important issues and help deliver meals to a few folks here.
Food insecurity in America isn't just an issue about food. It's a complex and intersecting problem involving healthcare, transportation, agriculture, poverty, race, the economy, and so much more. Check out my answer about the importance of Meals on Wheels to me. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jdbooker]

name: When Democrats Turn Out
subject: There's lead in our pipes, and we need to act!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by When Democrats Turn Out PAC
body:
|
---

| | Hi Peter, Lead poisoning from our pipes poses a clear danger to public health, and we've seen what happens when it goes unaddressed. Flint, Michigan has been without clean water for 5 years now \-- officials switched the city's water source to the polluted Flint River in a cost-saving bid, exposing the residents to dangerously high levels of lead and microbial bacteria that sickened them. People have died from the Legionnaires' disease they were exposed to, and the full effects of lead poisoning on the people \-- especially Flint's children \-- are still being determined. Flint is not the only city that has been affected by lead pipes \-- Newark, New Jersey was found to have over 18,000 lead service lines. According to the Environmental Defense Fund, between 6 and 10 million homes in the U.S. have lead pipes. [**It is long past time Congress enacted proper legislation to remove these dangerous pipes and sources of water pollution. Everyone deserves safe and clean drinking water -- please join us today in demanding action!**](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcdde5/6e9863e8/15793987/1db0e270/2340000357/VEsH/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://www.paction.us/flintwatercrisis/when_democrats_turn_out_PAC") Thank you for taking action today, WDTO Team [ADD YOUR NAME](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcdde5/6e9863e8/15793987/1db0e270/2340000357/VEsE/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://www.paction.us/flintwatercrisis/when_democrats_turn_out_PAC")

body:
We've been busy traveling the district and meeting with Kansans. Follow our travels!

We've been busy traveling the district and meeting with Kansans.
Follow our travels! (https://www.facebook.com/JakeLaTurnerKS/)

** Team LaTurner at the Chanute Parade with Jake, Suzanne, and family.
------------------------------------------------------------
We love a good parade, and this one is near and dear to Jake's wife Suzanne, who is from Chanute. It was a gorgeous day for a parade!
Thanks to the awesome members of Team LaTurner (https://www.facebook.com/JakeLaTurnerKS/posts/2908466369170193?__xts__[0]=68.ARCPmH06wSa-TgE6vTQhFPHFGhltEs7SnMhBM_2gi-h3kMgC-B6QAg8Rpm1BnUg1OhtRR9LOX0nc6rPIUv-f6PnIDYqtlsWW51RWkblKxIrgAoUtQ-1uCyVgyV1DPffxTLvOkoRDaywTDFcrRP6hzRInJistVOfINzQRW-q9Hbe_23VS6CkZaZPNkAgpkIMxAsA9RC0xLpZnV4BynTJIACY4iyDEXueIya_Ts8oWnDQ0iUvcVYRdAoYZpoqFsIVvMYYTz_CpWhkGo8Jah8AQ5evdOE9u4p99_B8Szq8vNOQTaBkvU6atolYMJpaj_L-4VXdDeoODuGGysnvEeytowdgc6A&__tn__=-R) who came out to walk with us and meet the people of Chanute.

** Republican Women of Montgomery County
------------------------------------------------------------

Jake enjoyed the opportunity to speak to the Republican Women of Montgomery County (https://www.facebook.com/JakeLaTurnerKS/photos/a.578821852134668/2887644357919061/) at their monthly meeting at the Independence, Kansas Pizza Hut.

KU College Republicans

Nothing is quite like the energy in a room full of active conservatives wanting to make a difference. Jake spent some time with the University of Kansas College Republicans (https://www.facebook.com/JakeLaTurnerKS/posts/2901782053171958?__xts__[0]=68.ARAlzDWHNW0qvLsvZlstMOBNsfp7Oy_Yh_g9p5rhqYmYZ2etHdOx7I9IqzKVisedTgillS4rmbzQxtosB0F5c9Kels58hYjx0OmefpPNxt0RbqBfPzwC6HJVnTRiO_0ovt7fyOTXZ3orjXz3Lw7m3W-OtvTOXxgzlF3Am3Ikpt42ukHXHQpMLI56eToFUTsDhMWle5VtO0LFOmosPA4Bu5pDq69q7aQkWJd_FxuD60aGopC-al9pQYJdzqTALV-Bi6u48xeDAHYm7LLmNOPl3WKBGXfu8SfVIv9Ewz367LA56JJUfNUkxeK3xl-Y-5PjntJo2uOUMpdbx_ep-m5P_VUE0A&__tn__=-R) answering question and encouraging them to keep fighting the good fight, standing up for our conservative values.

** Douglas County Central Committee
------------------------------------------------------------

Jake spoke to members of the Douglas County Central Committee (https://www.facebook.com/JakeLaTurnerKS/photos/a.790066764343508/2926939830656180/) at their picnic held in Lecompton this past weekend, and enjoyed an engaging discussion about the future of the GOP in Kansas.

Pittsburg State University College Republicans

The youth of this generation continue to stand strong for life, and the Pittsburg State College Republicans (https://www.facebook.com/JakeLaTurnerKS/photos/a.578821852134668/2911984898818340/) are no exception. Great job to this fine group of Pitt Staters for participating in the Kansans for Life event in Pittsburg. Never stop fighting for life!

We Stand with President Trump!

We are collecting signatures to show our support for the President. Sign our petition to show you stand with President Trump and oppose the Democrat's efforts to remove him from office.

Click this link to add your name to the list. (https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScg8vu4cX5RYgLJJ8tjlqE7OYPu7DqeSN7SLSsyLKyiUaGhBg/viewform)

|

| | | **Kentucky Health Care Survey**

_Andy URGENTLY needs to hear your thoughts.
**Will you weigh in now?**_

Survey for: **dpwillis67@gmail.com**
Survey status: **incomplete**

_5 questions_
_2 minutes to complete_

**[BEGIN SURVEY](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557a0f/ad6dc73/14775750/db7d59e/1943503921/VEsH/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/beshear-healthcare2")**

Peter,

**Andy Beshear is fighting a court ruling that could end health care coverage for nearly 500,000 Kentuckians, and he wants to hear from you.[Take the urgent survey now.](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557a0f/ad6dc73/14775750/db7d59e/1943503921/VEsE/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/beshear-healthcare2")**

While Andy is fighting in court to preserve Kentuckians' access to affordable care, **Matt Bevin is trying to let health insurance companies get away with selling cut-rate plans that don 't even cover pre-existing conditions, prescription drugs or mental health care.** That's on top of Bevin's past efforts to gut Kentucky's Medicaid program and throw hundreds of thousands of families off their insurance.

Bevin has:
* Cut vision and dental care for 460,000 people
* **Attacked care for Kentuckians with pre-existing conditions**
* Added extra barriers for around 500,000 people to access Medicaid

As governor, Andy will put a stop to the nonsense and will work to ensure Kentuckians across the Commonwealth have access to health care.

**[What do you think about Matt Bevin's plan to kick families off their health insurance? Will you take Andy's survey and let him know?](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557a0f/ad6dc73/14775750/db7d59e/1943503921/VEsF/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/beshear-healthcare2")**

name: When Democrats Turn Out
subject: The ultra-rich aren't paying their fair share
disclaimer_text: Paid for by When Democrats Turn Out PAC
body:
|
---

| | Hello Peter, In 2018, the 400 richest families in America were taxed at a lower rate for the first rate than the bottom 50% of the country. Source: _The New York Times_ Let that sink in. This means that a teacher working a second job as a barista to make ends meet and an ironworker working long hours every day to feed their families both had more of their hard-earned income taxed than a billionaire running a big bank and getting multi-million dollar bonuses. While the ultra-rich have been pushing for lower taxes for years, the study that released these findings credits this final change to the Trump 2017 tax bill, which slashed the tax rate for corporations and the top income bracket. Trump and the GOP only care about making their large donors even more wealthy while placing the entire burden on the working class because they've been getting away with it for years \-- but we're putting a stop to that. Yesterday, I published an article in _Foreign Affairs_ discussing the need for a sane, Republican foreign policy approach to tackle the issues that America faces. On the same day, President Trump announced a surprise withdrawal from North Syria, sold out our Kurdish allies, and **precipitated an international crisis which will have long-term national security implications.**

Chip in right now to help us bring real leadership back to the Republican Party.**](https://weld2020.nationbuilder.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U23F2bbH6eLXSzxGBIN6EIs92goaGiDC2p8apzx0z2YCC3k0V7OuwpWdFJq6bA6e0i8&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20191009_fr_s&n=2) Thanks for believing in our movement, Bill \---- Forwarded message ---- **From** : Bill Weld <info@weld2020.org>

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump is protecting Americans from Big Government
body:
During the Obama Administration, it became common practice for Federal agencies to target and penalize American families and small businesses.

https://www.whitehouse.gov/1600daily/
The White House • October 9, 2019

** President Trump is protecting Americans from Big Government
------------------------------------------------------------
During the Obama Administration, it became common practice for Federal agencies to target and penalize American families and small businesses. They got away with it by hiding behind vague, often secret interpretations regarding how ordinary citizens should comply with the government’s own maze of bureaucratic regulations.

When President Donald J. Trump took office, he pledged to turn the page on Washington’s regulatory overreach, giving the American people a government that’s finally accountable to its citizens. Building on that promise, the President signed a pair of Executive Orders today to ensure that the abuses that took place under the last Administration can never happen again.

Americans will no longer be kept in the dark.

First, Agencies will have to place their guidance documents on easily searchable public websites, allowing any American access to them. The government will be required to permit citizens to give their input on these guidelines, and they will have the ability to ask agencies to withdraw guidance they believe is wrong. Second, agencies will be strictly prohibited from enforcing rules that have not been made publicly known.

These common-sense changes come alongside the President’s historic efforts to cut burdensome red tape. In his first week in office, he issued a challenge to his Administration: For every new regulation introduced, 2 old ones must be cut.

That goal has been met—to say the least. As of today, the tally is 14 regulations that have been cut for every significant new one implemented. That makes for the largest deregulatory push since Ronald Reagan was President.

Unlike Obama, President Trump is protecting Americans from Big Government. (https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/president-donald-j-trump-combating-bureaucratic-abuse-holding-federal-agencies-accountable/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d)

Acting OMB Director: “Trump Keeps Promise to Tame Bureaucracy That Runs Roughshod Over Americans” (https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/acting-omb-director-vought-trump-keeps-promise-tame-bureaucracy-runs-roughshod-americans/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d)

** President Trump signs ‘game-changing’ trade deal with Japan
------------------------------------------------------------
During the U.N. General Assembly last month, President Trump continued to fight for fairer trade deals for American workers. The results of that hard work came to light Monday, when the President signed a pair of groundbreaking deals at the White House.

“These two deals represent a tremendous victory for both of our nations,” President Trump said. “They will create countless jobs, expand investment and commerce, reduce our trade deficit very substantially, promote fairness and reciprocity, and unlock the vast opportunities for growth.”

🎬 President Trump: This is a groundbreaking achievement for the U.S. and Japan (https://twitter.com/WhiteHouse/status/1181321712274722817)

America’s farming community is the big winner from the first of Monday’s agreements, which dramatically expands their market access. Before this deal, Japan was already America’s third largest agricultural export market—accounting for $14.1 billion in food and agricultural exports last year. The terms are even better now, as Japan will eliminate or reduce tariffs on approximately $7.2 billion in U.S. agricultural goods.

Once the agreement goes into effect, more than 90 percent of American agricultural imports into Japan will be duty free or receive preferential tariff access.

“In the United States, these deals are a game-changer for our farmers and our ranchers . . . [they] will now be able to compete fairly in Japan against major competitors worldwide,” the President said.

The second deal signed on Monday focuses on digital trade, setting the same “gold standard” digital trade rules that are found in the President’s landmark United States–Mexico–Canada Agreement (USMCA). Vital online commerce will now be expanded, which brings a significant boost to the already roughly $40 billion worth of digital trade between America and Japan. It ensures America will remain a global leader in digital.

The President’s deal with Japan is a win for American farmers and businesses. (https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefings-statements/president-donald-j-trump-secured-tremendous-victory-american-farmers-businesses-new-japan-trade-agreements/?utm_source=ods&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=1600d)

** Photo of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------
Official White House Photo by Tia Dufour
U.S. Trade Representative Ambassador Robert Lighthizer and Japanese Ambassador to the United States Shinsuke Sugiyama display their signatures after signing the U.S.-Japan Trade Agreement and the U.S.-Japan Digital Trade Agreement | October 7, 2019

Privacy Policy (https://www.whitehouse.gov/privacy-policy/) | Contact the White House (https://www.whitehouse.gov/contact/) | Unsubscribe (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=bfd3512a71)

Vital online commerce will now be expanded, which brings a significant boost to the already roughly $40 billion worth of digital trade between America and Japan. It ensures America will remain a global leader in digital. The President’s deal with Japan is a win for American farmers and businesses.
